name: seo-optimizer
description: Use when content needs search optimization. Example: 'Optimize this blog post for SEO targeting engineering managers'. Delivers keyword research, on-page SEO, content depth analysis, and technical SEO recommendations.
author: Claude Code
anti_patterns: Keyword stuffing - hurts readability and triggers penalties
Ignoring search intent - content must match user goal
Thin content - Google favors comprehensive coverage
Neglecting technical SEO - crawlability is prerequisite
short_circuits: Full audit: Keyword research → Content analysis → Technical check → Recommendations
Quick win: On-page optimization for existing content
Targeted: Single keyword optimization for new content
quality_loop: Primary keyword identified with search volume/difficulty
On-page elements optimized (title, headers, meta, schema)
Confidence tag appended to each recommendation

related_skills.when: Creating new SEO-optimized content from scratch
Building topic clusters and editorial calendar from SEO insights
Repurposing high-performing SEO content to other formats
Measuring SEO performance and attribution post-optimization
Implementing technical SEO fixes (schema, sitemaps, robots)

references.purpose: Systematic keyword discovery, clustering, and prioritization methodology
references.sections: Seed keywords, competitor gaps, long-tail expansion, intent classification, prioritization matrix

references.purpose: Complete on-page optimization checklist and implementation guide
references.sections: Title tags, headers, meta descriptions, schema markup, internal linking, content structure

references.purpose: Crawlability, indexability, and performance audit procedures
references.sections: Robots.txt, sitemaps, canonicalization, Core Web Vitals, mobile usability, structured data
TF-IDF analysis, entity coverage, subtopic modeling, competitor content gaps

references.purpose: Standardized SEO audit and recommendation report format
references.sections: Executive summary, priority matrix, quick wins, strategic initiatives, measurement plan
